_note: Top local models per task as of 2026. 'ollama' tags are pull strings; 'comfy' entries are ComfyUI checkpoints; provider='anthropic' picks call the Claude Messages API via the user's API key (set in Settings → API Keys).

picks.name: Claude Opus 4.8
picks.why: Anthropic's flagship — top-tier reasoning, code, and agentic tool use. Best for hard agent tasks where local models stall. Requires an Anthropic API key.

picks.name: Claude Sonnet 4.6
picks.why: Balanced cost/quality — fast enough for everyday chat, smart enough for serious agentic work. The default cloud pick. Requires an Anthropic API key.

picks.name: Claude Haiku 4.5
picks.why: Fastest Claude tier — quick conversational answers and low-latency tool calls. Requires an Anthropic API key.

picks.name: Gemini 3.1 Pro (Preview)
picks.why: Google's most advanced model — reasoning-first design optimized for complex agentic workflows and coding. Preview status. Requires a Google AI Studio API key.

picks.name: Gemini 3.5 Flash
picks.why: GA Flash model — the most intelligent Flash tier for sustained frontier performance on agentic and coding tasks. Great default cloud pick that balances cost and quality. Requires a Google AI Studio API key.

picks.name: Gemini 3.1 Flash-Lite
picks.why: The most cost-efficient Gemini — optimized for low-latency, high-volume traffic. Pick this when you want a cheap fast cloud answer. Requires a Google AI Studio API key.

picks.name: Qwen3 30B-A3B
picks.why: Frontier reasoning with MoE — only ~3B active params, so it's fast on a 24GB GPU while matching much larger dense models on benchmarks.

picks.name: Llama 3.3 70B Instruct
picks.why: Meta's strongest open instruct model — best-in-class general assistant quality. Quantized 4-bit fits on a 48GB card or dual-GPU; Q3 runs on 32GB.

picks.name: Qwen2.5 7B Instruct
picks.why: Alibaba's balanced 7B — strong instruction following, surprisingly good at coding and multilingual tasks, reliable tool use. ~5 GB runtime fits comfortably on an 8 GB Mac M2 Air at ~30–40 tok/s. The smart-but-portable middle tier between Llama 3.2 3B and the 30B+ heavyweights.

picks.name: Qwen3.5 9B Abliterated
picks.why: Alibaba's latest Qwen3.5 9B with the refusal direction surgically removed from the residual stream (abliteration) — keeps all of Qwen3.5's reasoning, multilingual, and tool-calling smarts but stops refusing. Outperforms older 13B models on most benchmarks; tool-capable so web search + file tools work. ~5.5 GB runtime, comfortable on a 16 GB Mac M2 Pro, tight on an 8 GB Air.

picks.name: Dolphin-Mistral 7B
picks.why: Eric Hartford's uncensored fine-tune of Mistral 7B — refusal data stripped from training. Less capable than Qwen3.5 Abliterated but lighter (~4 GB) and a known-good fallback if you want something even smaller. No tool/web-search support — Mistral 7B doesn't emit reliable structured tool calls.

picks.name: Llama 3.2 3B Instruct
picks.why: Tiny and fast — runs on a CPU or any GPU with 4 GB+. Great for quick questions, summaries and snappy back-and-forth when you don't need frontier reasoning. Does not support web search or other tools (model too small).

picks.name: Qwen2.5-VL 7B
picks.why: Best open vision-language model under 10B — strong OCR, charts, screenshots, document understanding. Auto-used when you attach an image to any text chat.

picks.name: Llama 3.2 Vision 11B
picks.why: Meta's multimodal instruct — strong general visual reasoning, captioning, visual Q&A. Auto-routed for images when the larger model fits.

picks.name: Qwen2.5-Coder 32B
picks.why: State-of-the-art open coding model — beats GPT-4o-mini on HumanEval/LiveCodeBench. Strong fill-in-middle for editor integrations.

picks.name: DeepSeek-Coder-V2 Lite 16B
picks.why: Sparse MoE (only 2.4B active) — extremely fast code generation and 338-language support. Great daily-driver pick.

picks.name: Qwen2.5-Coder 7B
picks.why: Same Qwen2.5-Coder family but small enough to run on an 8 GB card. Excellent quality-per-byte for autocomplete, snippets and quick fixes.

picks.name: Qwen3 30B-A3B
picks.why: Reliable JSON tool calls and native step-by-step reasoning when you enable Think mode. Best open model for multi-step agent loops at this size.

picks.name: Hermes 3 Llama 3.1 8B
picks.why: Tuned specifically for function calling, structured output, and agentic workflows. Light enough to keep loaded alongside another model.

picks.name: Qwen3 8B
picks.why: Same Qwen3 family as the 30B-A3B but at a fraction of the footprint — ~5 GB runtime. Native /think soft switch for step-by-step reasoning, strong tool-calling, reliable JSON output. Beats Hermes 3 8B on most function-calling benchmarks.

picks.name: Qwen2.5 3B Instruct
picks.why: The lightest tool-capable agent pick — ~2 GB runtime, snappy first-token latency on an 8 GB Mac Air where Qwen3 8B starts swapping. Qwen2.5 Instruct's tool-call grammar is well-supported by Ollama's templating, so web search, file reads, and shell commands work the same as on the bigger models. Trade-off vs Qwen3 8B: shorter chains-of-thought and less reliable multi-step planning, but for one-shot tool calls it's plenty.
